labels: Archive Pending transactions after set number of days

toggles.label: Auto-import
toggles.helpBlock: If you enable this setting (recommended), from now on, Synder will fetch all data from your payment/ecommerce platform. To get past data, please go to Import historical data page>>

toggles.label: Auto-sync
toggles.helpBlock: If enabled (recommended), from now on, all new transactions will be synced to your books automatically. If you disable Auto-import setting, Auto-sync won't work.

toggles.label: Skip synchronization of duplicated transactions
toggles.helpBlock: If enabled, the already existing transactions in your company will be skipped in the synchronization.

toggles.label: Process transactions in multiple currencies.
toggles.helpBlock: If disabled, Synder will only sync transactions matching your accounting platform home currency. Other transactions will be skipped.

toggles.label: Archive Pending transactions after set number of days
toggles.helpBlock: If enabled, Synder will automatically archive transactions that remain in Pending status for longer than the specified number of days.

toggles.label: Archive Pending transactions after set number of days
toggles.helpBlock: If enabled, Synder will automatically archive transactions that remain in Pending status for longer than the specified number of days.

toggles.label: Apply location
toggles.helpBlock: It is an option to assign a location to each transaction.

dropdowns.label: Location name
dropdowns.name: default.departmentName
dropdowns.helpBlock: Every transaction will be applied to this location.

dropdowns.label: Balance Reserve account
dropdowns.name: default.balanceReserveAccount.USD
dropdowns.helpBlock: Stripe reserves are funds that Stripe withholds/returns from/to a seller's payouts for various reasons. Learn more >>

helpBlocks: If you enable this setting (recommended), from now on, Synder will fetch all data from your payment/ecommerce platform. To get past data, please go to Import historical data page>>
If enabled (recommended), from now on, all new transactions will be synced to your books automatically. If you disable Auto-import setting, Auto-sync won't work.
If enabled, the already existing transactions in your company will be skipped in the synchronization.
If disabled, Synder will only sync transactions matching your accounting platform home currency. Other transactions will be skipped.
If enabled, Synder will automatically archive transactions that remain in Pending status for longer than the specified number of days.
It is an option to assign a location to each transaction.
Every transaction will be applied to this location.
Use this setting to post your Stripe payments not linked to invoices as Deposits or Sales Receipts. You can then apply deposits to invoices manually. Learn more >>
Stripe reserves are funds that Stripe withholds/returns from/to a seller's payouts for various reasons. Learn more >>
The imported transactions will be shown in the selected time zone. Make sure it matches the provider time zone for smooth reconciliation.
General.fullText:
Auto-import 
On
Off
 If you enable this setting (recommended), from now on, Synder will fetch all data from your payment/ecommerce platform. To get past data, please go to Import historical data page>>
Auto-sync 
On
Off
 If enabled (recommended), from now on, all new transactions will be synced to your books automatically. If you disable Auto-import setting, Auto-sync won't work.
Skip synchronization of duplicated transactions 
On
Off
 If enabled, the already existing transactions in your company will be skipped in the synchronization.
Process transactions in multiple currencies. 
On
Off
 If disabled, Synder will only sync transactions matching your accounting platform home currency. Other transactions will be skipped.
Archive Pending transactions after set number of days 
On
Off
 If enabled, Synder will automatically archive transactions that remain in Pending status for longer than the specified number of days.
Apply location 
On
Off
 Upgrade to use
 It is an option to assign a location to each transaction.

Sales receipts
Deposits
 Use this setting to post your Stripe payments not linked to invoices as Deposits or Sales Receipts. You can then apply deposits to invoices manually. Learn more >>

toggles.label: Enable QuickBooks Doc Numbers
toggles.helpBlock: By enabling this configuration your transactions will follow the standard QuickBooks Doc Number sequence.

toggles.label: Apply generic customer
toggles.helpBlock: It is an option to assign a generic customer instead of sending over each customer on sales transaction individually.

toggles.label: Sync discounts as products
toggles.helpBlock: Any discount will be synced as an additional product with default “Stripe discount” name or discount name from the integration (if any).

dropdowns.label: Clearing account
dropdowns.name: default.salesBankAccount.USD
dropdowns.helpBlock: Clearing account represents the payment processor in your books. All synced transactions will be deposited here. Learn more >>

dropdowns.label: Payment Method
dropdowns.name: default.paymentMethod
dropdowns.helpBlock: This Payment Method will be set for QuickBooks Sales Receipts, Refund Receipts, Payments and Expenses.

dropdowns.label: Generic customer
dropdowns.name: default.defaultCustomer
dropdowns.helpBlock: If the generic customer setting is “ON”, choose or type in a name to be used. All of your transactions will be synchronized under the default customer name.

dropdowns.label: Applied Balance Account
dropdowns.name: default.customerBalanceLiabilityAccount.USD
dropdowns.helpBlock: Used to record Stripe customer applied balances when they reduce an invoice or payment total. Synder uses this account to create and map the 'Stripe applied balance' item.

dropdowns.label: Discount product account
dropdowns.name: default.discountItemIncomeAccount.USD
dropdowns.helpBlock: Select an account from the books that will be used when creating a new discount product.

helpBlocks: Clearing account represents the payment processor in your books. All synced transactions will be deposited here. Learn more >>
This Payment Method will be set for QuickBooks Sales Receipts, Refund Receipts, Payments and Expenses.
Select posting date for transactions: Balance transaction date (recommended) - when the amount hits your Stripe balance, or Created date - when transaction is created in Stripe. Learn more
By enabling this configuration your transactions will follow the standard QuickBooks Doc Number sequence.
Drag and drop the lines to set your custom order of fields to take a payer's name from.
It is an option to assign a generic customer instead of sending over each customer on sales transaction individually.
If the generic customer setting is “ON”, choose or type in a name to be used. All of your transactions will be synchronized under the default customer name.
Used to record Stripe customer applied balances when they reduce an invoice or payment total. Synder uses this account to create and map the 'Stripe applied balance' item.
Any discount will be synced as an additional product with default “Stripe discount” name or discount name from the integration (if any).
Select an account from the books that will be used when creating a new discount product.

labels: Apply payments to unpaid Invoice/Bill transactions
Cancel sync if there is no matching open invoice found for a payment

toggles.label: Apply payments to unpaid Invoice/Bill transactions
toggles.helpBlock: Payment will be applied to the matching unpaid Invoice/Bill transaction.

toggles.label: Cancel sync if there is no matching open invoice found for a payment
toggles.helpBlock: If you enable this setting, Synder will search across the existing invoices in your books and if the matching invoice doesn't exist, it will cancel synchronization.

toggles.label: Sync unpaid (open) invoices
toggles.helpBlock: This setting allows you to sync open invoices with your accounting system. Once received, the payment will be automatically attached to the corresponding invoice and the invoice will be marked as paid. Learn more >>

helpBlocks: Payment will be applied to the matching unpaid Invoice/Bill transaction.
If you enable this setting, Synder will search across the existing invoices in your books and if the matching invoice doesn't exist, it will cancel synchronization.

This setting allows you to sync open invoices with your accounting system. Once received, the payment will be automatically attached to the corresponding invoice and the invoice will be marked as paid. Learn more >>
Enable this setting to import and sync invoices where all line amounts are 0 and the totals are 0. Learn more >>
Invoices.fullText:
Apply payments to unpaid Invoice/Bill transactions 
On
Off
 Payment will be applied to the matching unpaid Invoice/Bill transaction.
Cancel sync if there is no matching open invoice found for a payment 
On
Off
 If you enable this setting, Synder will search across the existing invoices in your books and if the matching invoice doesn't exist, it will cancel synchronization.
Sync unpaid (open) invoices 
On
Off
 This setting allows you to sync open invoices with your accounting system. Once received, the payment will be automatically attached to the corresponding invoice and the invoice will be marked as paid. Learn more >>
Sync zero invoices 
On
Off
 Enable this setting to import and sync invoices where all line amounts are 0 and the totals are 0. Learn more >>

labels: Get product name from a description of transaction or order

toggles.label: Inventory management
toggles.helpBlock: You can configure the application to create inventory products in your books. Synder will create a new inventory product if no product with the same name or SKU exists in your books.

toggles.label: Get product name from a description of transaction or order
toggles.helpBlock: When "ON", Synder will be taking a product name from a description of transaction or order.

dropdowns.label: Income account
dropdowns.name: default.inventoryItemIncomeAccount.USD
dropdowns.helpBlock: Track the sales of the inventory product on this account.

dropdowns.label: Inventory Asset account
dropdowns.name: default.itemAssetAccount.USD
dropdowns.helpBlock: This account will be used to track the cost of purchased products

dropdowns.label: Cost of goods sold account
dropdowns.name: default.inventoryItemExpenseAccount.USD
dropdowns.helpBlock: Cost of inventory items will be tracked on this account

dropdowns.label: Inventory adjustment account
dropdowns.name: default.inventoryAdjustmentAccount.USD
dropdowns.helpBlock: This account will be used to adjust inventory in case of shrinkage or different write-offs

dropdowns.label: Inventory start date
dropdowns.name: default.inventorySettings.startDateMode
dropdowns.selected: Use the transaction date as the inventory start date
dropdowns.helpBlock: Select the inventory start date that Synder will use when creating the new inventory product. Please note that posting sales of inventory products, which occurred before the set inventory start date will fail. Learn more >>

dropdowns.label: Initial Quantity on Hand
dropdowns.name: default.inventorySettings.quantityOnHandMode
dropdowns.selected: Use the quantity of sold items in the transaction during which the item is created
dropdowns.helpBlock: Select the initial Quantity on Hand that Synder will use when creating the new inventory product.

helpBlocks: Matching works automatically based on the product name or SKU.
You can configure the application to create inventory products in your books. Synder will create a new inventory product if no product with the same name or SKU exists in your books.
Track the sales of the inventory product on this account.
This account will be used to track the cost of purchased products
Cost of inventory items will be tracked on this account
This account will be used to adjust inventory in case of shrinkage or different write-offs

Select the inventory start date that Synder will use when creating the new inventory product. Please note that posting sales of inventory products, which occurred before the set inventory start date will fail. Learn more >>
Select the initial Quantity on Hand that Synder will use when creating the new inventory product.
When "ON", Synder will be taking a product name from a description of transaction or order.

errors: This feature is available on higher plans. Upgrade plan
helpBlocks:
If your product names match between your sales integration and your accounting integration, Synder will match them automatically. You can map your products here if you want to override this or if names don't match.

Map products
Product_mapping.fullText:
Product mapping
 If your product names match between your sales integration and your accounting integration, Synder will match them automatically. You can map your products here if you want to override this or if names don't match.

Map products

toggles.label: Apply Taxes
toggles.helpBlock: Tax details of your transactions will be recorded as an additional product line in QuickBooks.

helpBlocks: Tax details of your transactions will be recorded as an additional product line in QuickBooks.

Tax details of your transactions will be transferred over to your books, and QuickBooks will apply a tax code based on your Tax center settings. If you don’t see any tax information imported to your books, have Synder apply taxes with Smart rules >>
Taxes.fullText:
Apply Taxes 
On
Off
 Tax details of your transactions will be transferred over to your books, and QuickBooks will apply a tax code based on your Tax center settings. If you don’t see any tax information imported to your books, have Synder apply taxes with Smart rules >>
Sales tax settings
 Default tax code
10.0% (10%)
5.6% (5.6%)
7.0% (7%)
8.688% (8.688%)
9.75% (9.75%)
California (8%)
Tucson (9.1%)
 Apply generic tax code 
On
Off
Need more flexibility with tax settings? Build a Smart Rule >>

Multicurrency.fullText:
Synder differentiates between your home currency (based on your company currency settings) and foreign currencies.
Before you start syncing foreign currency transactions, make sure that multicurrency is enabled in your QuickBooks. Learn how to enable it here.

To set up additional currencies follow these simple steps:

Step 1: Go to Sales and press on Gear

Step 2: Choose currencies and Set up a Deposit account
Step 3: Select Fee Bank/Credit account and Fee category
Step 4: Choose Expense Bank/Credit account and Expense category
Step 5: Save your changes by clicking on Update
